ref: The Lord replied, “No, he won't! You will have a son of your own, and everything you have will be his.”
text: Belaa o berɛ no kɛŋɔ Korowii aa bol waa o be ja ŋɔ, “N dɛ ŋɔ he be ŋɔ a ɡɛ bonɔɔ nɔ laa dɛ́ di he kapɛrɛ, he ɡbaɡba biee dɛ́ di.”
translated: When the chief of the town called Korowii and asked him, he said, "I am here because of the matter that has been troubling me for a long time, and I want to end it."

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Ola baa fuah la sɔɔh do Korowii som kohona asɛ o ŋɔ, “Nɛwɛnyini le o wechɔɡa ne o bechale saŋ, anomanta, maa nɛwɛnyina ne komɛlbwa maa taa chɔɡ ɡɛ maa tɔ sie chɔɡ bane la kpa.”
translated: The elders of the Korowai people say this, "If you follow the ways of your ancestors and live a good life, then you will be blessed with long life, prosperity, and you will see your children and grandchildren grow up to have children of their own."

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: Maa sɛh lɛ komɛlbwa nemenɛ anɔ la bonɔɔna la aa dia la. Baala baa ja n nɛɛ dɛ la laanee chɛ kɛŋɔ he tɛɛ ba ba bɔr. Banee Aner ne Ɛskɔl dɛ Mamre.”
translated: The next day, the people went to the house of the chief and ate there. They said that they would not leave until he told them the truth. They gave the name of that place Bethel, but formerly it was called Mamre.

ref: the third was Basemath, who was Ishmael's daughter and Nebaioth's sister.
text: O hantoorote sɔne Basemat, o mɛɛ sɔne Ismael, o nimambie sɔne Nɛbayɔt.
translated: The firstborn son of Basemat, the second son of Ismael, and the third son of Nebayot.

ref: Then the Lord said, “I won't let my life-giving breath remain in anyone forever. No one will live for more than
text: Anee Korowii ŋɔ, “Maa ɡel n Doma waa dɛ tɛ mkpa la do nɛwɛnyini bini saŋ bɛlbwa dɛɛ la. Baa wonoo sewɛ. Taraken, nɛn aa biɡra kwaraa la, anee bene lafa fifraanɛ ɡbo, dɛ o sewɔ.”
translated: And Korowi said, "I am going to Doma to see the reason why the people are suffering from hunger. It is very bad. Therefore, if I do not return, you should take care of my wife."
